title.en: Machine Screws Complete Guide: Specifications, Head Types & Industrial Applications
title.zh: 机用螺钉完整指南：规格、头型与工业应用

description.en: Complete guide to machine screws covering specifications, head types, and industrial applications. Learn the difference between machine screws and sheet metal screws for precision fastening in African industrial settings.
description.zh: 机用螺钉完整指南，涵盖规格、头型和工业应用。了解机用螺钉与金属板螺钉的区别，适用于非洲工业环境的精密紧固。
description.es: Guía completa de tornillos para máquina que cubre especificaciones y tipos de cabeza.
description.ar: دليل شامل لبراغي الآلات يغطي المواصفات وأنواع الرؤوس.
description.fr: Guide complet des vis machine couvrant les spécifications et les types de tête.
description.pt: Guia completo de parafusos de máquina cobrindo especificações e tipos de cabeça.

keywords: machine screws, machine screw specifications, industrial fasteners, precision fastening, DIN 963, DIN 964

body.en: Machine screws are precision fasteners with internal threads designed to mate with a pre-tapped hole or nut. Unlike self-tapping screws, machine screws require a pre-drilled pilot hole with matching threads. They are typically made to tighter tolerances than sheet metal screws and are used in precision applications where alignment and thread fit are critical.

Machine screws come in various head types: flat head (countersunk) sits flush with the material surface; round head provides a decorative appearance with good bearing surface; pan head offers high tightening torque capability; and button head (dome head) provides a rounded appearance with good strength.

Machine screws are available in metric sizes from M2 to M10 and in lengths from 3mm to 100mm. Thread types include coarse (standard) and fine pitch threads. Materials range from zinc-plated steel for basic applications to stainless steel A2 for corrosion resistance and brass for electrical applications.

Machine screws are used in African industry: electrical panels—securing components in switchgear and distribution boards; machinery assembly—precision fastening in manufacturing equipment; telecommunications—mounting equipment in telecom shelters; and automotive—general assembly and repair applications.

heading.en: Machine Screws vs. Sheet Metal Screws

body.en: The key difference: machine screws require pre-tapped holes and nuts; sheet metal screws create their own threads. Machine screws offer better precision and can be repeatedly assembled/disassembled; sheet metal screws offer faster installation but can strip threads. Use machine screws when precision and repeated assembly are needed; use sheet metal screws for quick fastening in thin materials.
